stem: A banking study surveys 15 approved loans and observes 2 positive outcomes out of 15 (13.3%). The sample contains two subgroups: Group A (6 observations, rate = 16.7%) and Group B (9 observations, rate = 11.1%). The true population composition is uncertain: Group A makes up between 35% and 46% of the population. Estimate the true loan default rate and provide a 95% confidence interval.

ground_truth.answer: Simple pooled estimate: 0.1333. Weighted estimate (stratified): 0.0914 (range [0.089, 0.0943]). Combined CI accounting for stratification uncertainty: [0.0373, 0.3788]. Point estimate: 0.0914.

solution_steps: Stratified estimate: weighted by population composition. At w_A=0.41: 0.41×0.063 + 0.59×0.1111 = 0.0914.
common_failure_modes: Using the Wald interval (p̂ ± z√(p̂(1-p̂)/n)) which performs poorly for small samples
Not reporting the confidence interval alongside the point estimate
Not recognizing that with n=15, the normal approximation may be poor
Ignoring the population composition uncertainty and using the simple pooled rate
